title: poa caerulea var. atrovirens
intro: Poa caerulea var. atrovirens, also known as Blue Fescue Grass, is a type of perennial grass belonging to the Poaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat digestive issues and inflammation, making it a valuable resource in traditional herbal medicine. The health benefits of Poa caerulea var. atrovirens include its potential to soothe gastrointestinal problems, reduce fever, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the grass is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
